title: ceraia carinata
intro: Ceraia carinata, also known as the 'Wild Lime', is a tropical plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of limonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Ceraia carinata has been reported to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
